question: What was the deadline for written comments on the proposed rule?

reference_answer: From the second floor to the third floor of 1400 K Street NW, Washington, DC

question: According to the rule, what method does FSIP wish to strongly encourage parties to use for filing permissible documents?
reference_answer: The eFiling system on the FLRA's website (eFiling)

question: How far in advance must a party schedule an appointment for in-person delivery to the FSIP?
reference_answer: At least one business day in advance of submission

question: On what days is in-person delivery accepted, by appointment only?
reference_answer: Monday through Friday, except Federal holidays

question: What facsimile number is listed for the FSIP office?
List two methods, other than electronic filing, by which requests may be submitted to the Panel.
reference_answer: Any two of: registered mail, certified mail, regular mail, commercial delivery, facsimile, in-person delivery (with advance permission/appointment)

question: What is the street address of the Office of the Executive Director, Federal Service Impasses Panel?
reference_answer: Suite 300, 1400 K Street NW, Washington, DC 20424-0001

question: Which five sections of the CFR did the FSIP propose to amend?
According to the rule, when service is made by electronic or facsimile transmission, what is the date of service?
What is the statutory authority citation for Part 2471?
What is the statutory authority citation for Part 2472?
According to the Regulatory Flexibility Act Certification, why does the rule not have a significant impact on a substantial number of small entities?
reference_answer: Because this final rule applies only to Federal agencies, Federal employees, and labor organizations representing those employees

question: Why is the FLRA not subject to the requirements of Executive Order 12866, according to this rule?
reference_answer: Because the FLRA is an independent regulatory agency

question: Why is the FLRA not subject to the requirements of Executive Order 13132, according to this rule?
reference_answer: Because the FLRA is an independent regulatory agency

question: According to the rule, does this final rule contain any additional information collection or recordkeeping requirements under the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1995?
reference_answer: No; the final rule contains no additional information collection or recordkeeping requirements under the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1995

question: What three "Subjects" are listed for 5 CFR Parts 2471 and 2472?
reference_answer: Administrative practice and procedure; Government employees; Labor management relations

question: Who signed the rule as Solicitor, Federal Register Liaison?
On what date was the rule approved (per the signature block)?
What was the filing date stamped on the Federal Register document, as indicated in the FR Doc filing line?
reference_answer: 3-25-24 (March 25, 2024); the FR Doc was filed at 8:45 am

question: According to § 2471.2, what alternative does the rule provide if a party does not use the official form?
reference_answer: Use of the form is not required, provided the request includes all of the information set forth in § 2471.3

question: According to § 2472.5, what two methods may be used to file requests to the Panel under that part?
reference_answer: Filed electronically through the FLRA's eFiling system on www.flra.gov, or addressed to the Executive Director, Federal Service Impasses Panel, Suite 300, 1400 K Street NW, Washington, DC 20424-0001

question: What does the rule cite as the billing code at the bottom?
According to the rule, what kinds of impasses does Part 2472 specifically address?
reference_answer: Impasses arising pursuant to agency determinations not to establish or to terminate flexible or compressed work schedules
